Japanese: １月　７日　午前　９時４８分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: January 7, 9:48 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: そッスか‥‥やっぱり‥‥
English: Oh... I see... I guess I should've expected this.

Japanese: ダレも見てないんですね。 あの、２人目のオトコ。
English: Nobody saw the other guy, huh?

Japanese: アタシがコーヒーを運んだときは ‥‥たしかに、いたのに！
English: But he was there when I took the coffee over, sir. Scout's honor!

Japanese: マコクンッ！
English: Maggey!

Japanese: ‥‥あッ！ い‥‥、イトノコセンパイ！
English: Ack! D-Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: だ‥‥大丈夫ッスか！ げ‥‥元気ッスか！
English: A-Are you doing alright!? How're you feeling!?

Japanese: ‥‥両方とも、 ビミョーな質問ッス。
English: ...As if you need to ask either question, sir.

Japanese: カラダに気をつけて、 ちゃんと食べなきゃダメッスよ！
English: Don't let 'em get you down, Maggey. And don't forget to eat well, OK?

Japanese: リョーカイッス！
English: Roger!

Japanese: そして、コラ！　アンタ！
English: And you!

Japanese: は、はい！
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: モギ取るッスよ！　無罪判決！
English: You'd better square this case away, got it pal!? Maggey's innocent, you hear!?

Japanese: シッパイしたら、かわりに 自分が、モギ取るッス！
English: If you screw up, then I'll be doing some squaring away myself.

Japanese: ‥‥アンタの首をッ！
English: Squaring away some paperwork for your arrest!

Japanese: （こ、この目‥‥ホンキだ！）
English: (I-I think he's serious!)

Japanese: ね。イトノコさん！　今日は、 あたしたちの味方なんですよね！
English: Hey, Detective! You're on our side for once, right?

Japanese: え。
English: Yup.

Japanese: マコちゃんのコト、 助けてくれるんでしょう？
English: So you'll be able to help Maggey out, right?

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか！ イトノコセンパイ！
English: Really? Can you, sir!?

Japanese: も‥‥‥モチロンッス！ センパイにまかせるッス！
English: O... Of course! I've got the situation under control!

Japanese: いッスね！　自分は今日、 最初の証人ッス！
English: I'm gonna be the first witness on the stand today.

Japanese: キチンと見つけるッスよ！ 致命的なムジュンを！
English: If something I say doesn't mesh with the facts, make sure you point it out, alright!?

Japanese: わ、わかりました。
English: S-Sure.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃッ！　今日は 共同戦線ッス！
English: OK. We're forming a united front today, pal. You get me!?

Japanese: ‥‥スズキ、カンゲキッス！
English: I can't tell you how grateful I am, sir!

Japanese: イトノコセンパイこそ、スズキが アコガレた刑事（デカ）ッス！
English: I've always admired you so much, Detective.

Japanese: さっすが、たのもしいなあ。
English: I know I can count on you.

Japanese: なんとかなりそうだね。 今日の法廷。
English: Looks like it should all go pretty smoothly today, huh?

Japanese: （だといいけど‥‥）
English: (I can only wish...)

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第４法廷
English: January 7, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 4

Japanese: これより、須々木　マコの 法廷を開廷します。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Maggey Byrde.

Japanese: 弁護側、準備完了しております。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.
Aaah, bitter!

Japanese: あの‥‥な、成歩堂さん？
English: Mr... Um... Mr. Wright.

Japanese: は、はい。なんでしょう？
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: うおおおッ！
English: Aaaah!

Japanese: ど、どうしましたか？
English: Wh-What's wrong!?

Japanese: いや、この前の法廷で あなたに呼びかけたら‥‥、
English: Nothing. It's just whenever I addressed you in the previous trial, your response was...

Japanese: “どないやねんッ！”
English: "Youse talkin' to me!?"

Japanese: と、ドヤしつけられて‥‥ホントに その、コワかったものですから。
English: It was a little, well... intimidating.

Japanese: いやいやいや！　だからそれは、 ニセモノだったんですよ。
English: No, no! That wasn't me. That was the phony Phoenix.

Japanese: 今度こそ、私たちの成歩堂くんが 帰ってきたのですね。
English: I see. So our trusty Phoenix Wright is back with us now, is he?
("Our trusty"...?)

Japanese: では、ゴドー検事。 冒頭弁論をおねがいします。
English: So, Mr. Godot. Your opening statement, please.

Japanese: まるほどう‥‥。アンタが ホンモノか、ニセモノか‥‥
English: Mr. Trite... Whether you're a fake or the real deal...

Japanese: 今日の法廷で ハッキリするだろうぜ。
English: We will find out soon enough through this trial today.

Japanese: い、いやいや。ぼくは ホンモノの成歩堂　龍一です！
English: But I can already tell you! I'm the real Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: オレが言ってるのは、 そんなコトじゃねえさ‥‥
English: I wasn't questioning whether you are Phoenix Wright or not.

Japanese: そもそも“まるほどう”という オトコが、ホンモノなのか‥‥？
English: I was questioning whether you had studied law or not.

Japanese: オレに関心があるのは、 それだけ、だぜ。
English: That's what I intend to find out.

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥感じるな）
English: (There's no denying it.)

Japanese: （あの仮面のウラから‥‥ 強い“敵意”を！）
English: (Behind that mask is a man who really hates me for some reason or another!)

Japanese: ご存じのとおり、この審理は 一度、判決が下されています。
English: As everyone is aware, the court has already given its verdict on this case once.

Japanese: したがって、今回は、 よけいな回り道は認めません。
English: Therefore, I won't stand for irrelevant testimony during this retrial.

Japanese: 同じ話は聞きたくありませんので。
English: Nor will I stand for a simple repetition of the evidence presented in the last trial.

Japanese: （こっちだって、 同じ話にはさせないぞ‥‥）
English: (I'm not planning on repeating anything that phony me said! Trust me!)

Japanese: ‥‥では、さっそく最初の 証人を呼んでいただきましょう。
English: Now then. Mr. Godot, please summon your first witness.

Japanese: 社交辞令として聞くぜ。 ‥‥名前と職業を。
English: Let's start with the formalities, shall we? Name and occupation?

Japanese: 証人！　お名前！
English: Witness! State your name for the court!

Japanese: あッ！　す、すまねッス！
English: Huh? Oh, sorry, sir!

Japanese: 名前は所轄署の刑事ッス。 糸鋸　圭介やってるッス。
English: The name's Police Department Detective. Occupation, Dick Gumshoe.

Japanese: ‥‥逆だ、刑事。
English: Other way around, Detective.

Japanese: あれッ！　そッスか！
English: Huh? Oh! Sorry.

Japanese: と、とにかく自分は、きのうから この事件の担当になったッス！
English: Anyway, I'm the officer in charge of this case since yesterday. Sir!

Japanese: きのうから‥‥？
English: Since yesterday?

Japanese: 初動捜査を担当した刑事は、 ベツの件で忙しいそうッス。
English: Yeah. The guy who was on the initial investigation's tied up with another case now.

Japanese: 大丈夫かな、イトノコさん。
English: I hope Gumshoe's really got everything under control... For everyone's sake.

Japanese: それでは‥‥証人。
English: I see. So, Detective Gumshoe...

Japanese: 事件の基本的なデータを カクニンしておきましょうか。
English: Would you outline for the court the basic facts of the case?

Japanese: はは‥‥は、ははは、はッ。
English: Y-Y-Yes, sir.

Japanese: 被害者は岡　高夫。 プロのプログラマーッス。
English: The victim's name was Glen Elg. He was a professional programmer.

Japanese: コンピュータソフトの開発会社 《バグダス》の社員ッス。
English: He was on the payroll of Blue Screens Inc., a local company.

Japanese: これが、被害者の解剖記録ッス。
English: This is the victim's autopsy report.

Japanese: ‥‥受理しましょう。
English: The court accepts this into evidence.

Japanese: 証拠品《解剖記録》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Glen's Autopsy Report added to the Court Record.

Japanese: えー。では、 上面図を見ていただくッス。
English: Um... And here are the floor plans of the restaurant.

Japanese: 事件が起こったとき、 被害者はこの席に座っていたッス。
English: When the incident took place, the victim was sitting right here.

Japanese: 毒が入っていたコーヒーは、その。 ウエイトレスさんが運んだッス。
English: The poisoned coffee was brought over to him by the, um... By the waitress.

Japanese: ウエイトレス‥‥当然、 被告人のコトですな。
English: The waitress being the accused?

Japanese: ‥‥そッス。
English: Yeah...

Japanese: コーヒーをヒトクチ飲んだ 被害者は、すぐに中毒死。
English: The victim died from poisoning almost immediately after he took a sip of the coffee.

Japanese: そのとき、他に店内にいたのは‥‥
English: At the time of the incident, there were two other people in the restaurant.

Japanese: シェフ・本土坊　薫。
English: Mr. Jean Armstrong, the owner and chef.

Japanese: そして、常連客の 五十嵐　将兵。‥‥この２人ッス。
English: And a regular by the name of Mr. Victor Kudo.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: やはり、明白な事件であるように 思われますね‥‥。
English: It still seems to be a very straightforward case to me.

Japanese: 証拠品《吐麗美庵の上面図》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Trés Bien Floor Plans added to the Court Record.

Japanese: じゃあ、刑事。アンタの手で、 打ちこんでやってくれ。
English: Come, Detective. Take up this hammer.

Japanese: ‥‥被告人のカンオケに、 最後のクギを、な。
English: And nail the defendant's coffin shut with your own two hands.

Japanese: それでは‥‥ 証言をおねがいします！
English: Now then, Detective Gumshoe. Let's have your testimony.

Japanese: ううう‥‥了解ッス。
English: Umm... Yes, sir...

Japanese: ～事件について～
English: -- The Incident --

Japanese: 事件が起こったとき、テーブルには 被害者しかいなかったッス。
English: When the incident took place, the victim was alone at his table, sir.

Japanese: 岡　高夫は、どうやら ラジオを聞いていたらしいッス。
English: We understand that the guy, Glen Elg, was listening to the radio at the time.

Japanese: 毒は‥‥コーヒーカップの中から 検出されたッス。
English: Traces of poison were found in his coffee cup.

Japanese: 青酸カリ。これはもう、 エラいイキオイで猛毒ッス。
English: And what we found was potassium cyanide. That stuff really packs a punch!

Japanese: さらに‥‥被告人には、その。 動機っぽいものもあったッス。
English: And, um... it looks like Ms. Byrde might've had, well... some kind of a motive.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: カップの中の闇に毒をかくす‥‥ シャレたおじょうさんだぜ。
English: Using the dark, aromatic depths of coffee to conceal the poison... Classy lady!

Japanese: これはもう、 疑いの余地がありませんな。
English: The facts of this case seem to be ironclad.

Japanese: それでは、成歩堂くん。 尋問をおねがいしますが‥‥
English: Mr. Wright. I would ask you to begin your cross-examination, but...

Japanese: はい？
English: Yes?

Japanese: こないだみたいに、ワレワレを ドナりつけないように。
English: Please, no intimidation tricks this time around. Is that understood?

Japanese: ‥‥だから、それは ぼくじゃありませんよ！
English: I already told you! That wasn't me!

Japanese: ～事件について～
English: -- The Incident --

Japanese: 事件が起こったとき、テーブルには 被害者しかいなかったッス。
English: When the incident took place, the victim was alone at his table, sir.

Japanese: 岡　高夫は、どうやら ラジオを聞いていたらしいッス。
English: We understand that the guy, Glen Elg, was listening to the radio at the time.

Japanese: 毒は‥‥コーヒーカップの中から 検出されたッス。
English: Traces of poison were found in his coffee cup.

Japanese: 青酸カリ。これはもう、 エラいイキオイで猛毒ッス。
English: And what we found was potassium cyanide. That stuff really packs a punch!

Japanese: さらに‥‥被告人には、その。 動機っぽいものもあったッス。
English: And, um... it looks like Ms. Byrde might've had, well... some kind of a motive.

Japanese: こ、この証言‥‥ ムジュンなんて、あるのかな？
English: You really think there are any contradictions in this testimony?

Japanese: 正直なトコロ‥‥ わからないね。
English: To be honest, I don't know.

Japanese: だって、イトノコさん、 控え室で言ってたじゃない！
English: But Gumshoe told us out in the lobby.

Japanese: 『今日は共同戦線だ』って！
English: He said we'd be forming a united front, right?

Japanese: コマるなあ。ちゃんと ズルしてくれなきゃ。
English: How will we win the case if he doesn't throw us a line?

Japanese: （しかたがない‥‥）
English: (I don't have a whole lot of options right now...)

Japanese: （とにかく今は、情報を 集めるしかないな‥‥）
English: (The best I can do is gather the facts together, I guess.)

Japanese: ちょっと、いいですか？
English: Can I stop you for just a minute?

Japanese: おッ！　な、なんスか！ ムジュンしちゃったッス？　自分。
English: Huh? What is it? Did I say something that contradicts the evidence?

Japanese: （‥‥キタイに 目をうるませているぞ‥‥）
English: (He's so desperate for that to be true, he's practically crying!)

Japanese: 今の証言‥‥被告人の主張と ムジュンしています。
English: Your testimony just now doesn't match the testimony given by Ms. Byrde.

Japanese: 被告人は、テーブルに もう１人オトコがいた、と‥‥
English: She claims that there was another man at the victim's table.

Japanese: いやあ、そッスよねえ！ ジツは、自分も‥‥
English: Yeah, that's what she said. And I--

Japanese: 殺人罪を逃れるためだ。 それぐらいのコトは言うだろうぜ。
English: What killer wouldn't say that when faced with a homicide conviction?

Japanese: なんですって‥‥！
English: Hey!

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥シェフと 常連客の証言は、そうじゃねえ。
English: Sadly, her testimony isn't supported by the owner or the other customer.

Japanese: そうだな？　刑事‥‥
English: Isn't that right, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥２人の証言は 一致してるッス。
English: Yeah, it's true. Their two testimonies tie up on that.

Japanese: あのテーブルには、 ダレもいなかった、と‥‥
English: They both said there was no other guy at the table.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: （どうする‥‥？ もっとつっこんでみるか？）
English: (What should I do? Should I press on this point a little harder or not?)

Japanese: （いや。やめておこう。 イヤな予感がする‥‥）
English: (No, I should leave it alone. I've got a bad feeling about where this is going.)

Japanese: イヤな予感って、 たいてい当たるよねー。
English: If you've got a bad feeling about this, then maybe you should leave it alone.

Japanese: あたしなんか、イヤな予感が する予感がするコトがあるよ。
English: I once had a bad feeling that I was about to have a bad feeling. It was really bad.

Japanese: ‥‥とりあえず、他のコトを 聞いてみるよ。
English: ...Maybe I should move on to another part of his testimony.

Japanese: もしかしたら、証人たちが 見落としたのかもしれません！
English: Well, maybe the other witnesses just missed him!

Japanese: 何かのカゲで、被害者の テーブルが見えなかったのかも‥‥
English: Perhaps their view of the victim's table was obscured in some way.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ カナしすぎるぜ‥‥まるほどう。
English: Ha...! That argument is as weak as the coffee at Trés Bien, Trite!

Japanese: ここに‥‥ チケットがあるぜ。
English: I have here in my possession a ticket.

Japanese: ちけっと‥‥？ （写真じゃないか‥‥）
English: A ticket? (Looks more like a photo to me.)

Japanese: ‥‥有罪判決行きのチケット。 アンタたちへのプレゼントさ！
English: Yes. A one-way ticket to guilty-ville! Population: the defendant!

Japanese: こいつは、キッチンの入り口 付近から撮影したものだ。
English: This is a photograph taken from near the entrance to the kitchen.

Japanese: 事件当時、シェフが 目撃した光景、というわけですな。
English: This is the scene as witnessed by the chef, moments after the poisoning took place, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥これだけハッキリ 現場が見えてるってのに‥‥
English: I think the court will agree that with such a clear view of the scene of the crime...

Japanese: どうやったら、ヒトひとり 見落とせると言うつもりだッ？
English: ...how, Mr. Trite, could anyone have overlooked a second person at the table?

Japanese: ‥‥ぐお！
English: Urk!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥、被害者のテーブルは イヤでも目に入りますな。
English: It certainly seems to show the victim's table extremely clearly.

Japanese: 証拠品《現場写真》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Crime Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ラジオ‥‥ですか。
English: He was listening to his radio, you say?

Japanese: 胸のポケットに、小型の ラジオが入っていたッス。
English: Yeah. He had a portable radio in his chest pocket.

Japanese: マコちゃんも言ってたよね。
English: Maggey told us that too, didn't she?

Japanese: 『被害者は、イヤホンで 何か聞いてた』って。
English: Something about how "one of them had some sort of earpiece".

Japanese: （どうしよう‥‥。 もっとつっこんでみるか？）
English: (What should I do? Should I press on this point a little harder or not?)

Japanese: （いや。やめておこう。 イヤな予感がする‥‥）
English: (No, I should leave it alone. I've got a bad feeling about where this is going.)

Japanese: イヤな予感って、 たいてい当たるよねー。
English: If you've got a bad feeling about this, then maybe you should leave it alone.

Japanese: あたしなんか、イヤな予感が する予感がするコトがあるよ。
English: I once had a bad feeling that I was about to have a bad feeling. It was really bad.

Japanese: ‥‥とりあえず、他のコトを 聞いてみるよ。
English: ...Maybe I should move on to another part of his testimony.

Japanese: 被害者は、ラジオで 何を聞いてたんですか？
English: And what was it that the victim was listening to on the radio, Detective Gumshoe?
Huh?

Japanese: そんなの、自分が 知ってるワケがないッス！
English: How should I know?

Japanese: （イバられた‥‥）
English: (Thanks a lot... We're now one step closer to the middle of nowhere.)

Japanese: イミ、なかったね。
English: This isn't going very well, is it...?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥では、殺害に使われた 毒について、証言してください。
English: Hmm... Detective. Could you perhaps tell us about the poison and how it was used?

Japanese: 毒が発見されたのは、 コーヒーカップだけですか？
English: So, traces of the poison were found in the coffee cup and nowhere else?

Japanese: どういうことッスか？
English: Not sure I get you, pal.

Japanese: その“毒”‥‥液体ですか？ それとも、粉末なんですか？
English: Was the poison a liquid? Or was it a powder?

Japanese: シロートにもわかりやすく 言うなれば、まあ‥‥粉末ッス。
English: If I had to put it in layman's terms, I'd say it was a powdery substance.

Japanese: それならば、サトウとかシオとか、 コショウのビンにだって、毒は‥‥
English: So the poison could've been in anything that was on the table. Sugar, salt, pepper...

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
コーヒーにシオ、コショウで 味つけするのかい‥‥アンタは。
English: Do you put salt and pepper in your coffee, Trite?

Japanese: ちなみに被害者は、 ブラックで飲んでいたッス。
English: The victim took his coffee black, with no sugar.

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Nngh...

Japanese: やはり‥‥毒はコーヒーに 入れられたようですね。
English: It seems that the poison could only have been in the coffee.

Japanese: （どうしよう‥‥。 もっとつっこんでみるか？）
English: (What should I do? Should I press on this point a little harder or not?)

Japanese: （いや。やめておこう。 イヤな予感がする‥‥）
English: (No, I should leave it alone. I've got a bad feeling about where this is going.)

Japanese: イヤな予感って、 たいてい当たるよねー。
English: If you've got a bad feeling about this, then maybe you should leave it alone.

Japanese: あたしなんか、イヤな予感が する予感がするコトがあるよ。
English: I once had a bad feeling that I was about to have a bad feeling. It was really bad.

Japanese: ‥‥とりあえず、他のコトを 聞いてみるよ。
English: ...Maybe I should move on to another part of his testimony.

Japanese: 被害者がコーヒーを飲んだ というのは、たしかなんですか？
English: Are you absolutely certain that the victim even drank any of his coffee?

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうイミッス？
English: Huh!? What do you mean?

Japanese: “コーヒーカップに 毒が入っていた”‥‥
English: According to this file, "the poison was found in the victim's coffee cup."

Japanese: しかし！　被害者がそれを 飲んだという証拠は、ありません！
English: But what proof is there that the victim ever drank any of it!?

Japanese: あっ！　ナルホド！ ジツは、自分も‥‥
English: Oh, hey! You're right! That's what I was think--

Japanese: ‥‥ちなみに、今のは そこの刑事にぶつけた異議だぜ。
English: In case you were wondering, that last objection was for the detective there.

Japanese: え！　自分ッスか！
English: Huh? For me?

Japanese: “あっ！　ナルホド！” じゃねえだろう、刑事！
English: "Oh, hey! You're right!"? You may be fooling the court, but I'm not falling for it.

Japanese: ナットクするヒマがあったら、 さっさとアレを提出してくれ。
English: If you have the time to waste, you have the time to present that piece of evidence.

Japanese: あ‥‥アレ‥‥ッスか‥‥！
English: Th... THAT piece... sir?

Japanese: そう。アレだ。
English: Yes. THAT piece.

Japanese: あ、あの。 なんでしたッスっけ。アレって。
English: Um... Aheh heh heh! Wh-What piece was it again?

Japanese: コイツだッ！
English: This!

Japanese: （なんで、ぼくなんだ‥‥）
English: (Should I be grateful this coffee's only hot enough to give me 1st degree burns...?)

Japanese: あ。思い出したッス！
English: Oh. Now I remember!

Japanese: ええと‥‥。被害者が使った コーヒーカップッス。
English: Um... This is the, uh... victim's coffee cup.

Japanese: このカップ‥‥ フチをよく見てみな。
English: Yes, the victim's cup. Take a good look at the rim.

Japanese: ハッキリ残っていますね。 ‥‥コーヒーを飲んだ跡が。
English: Oh, yes. It's unmistakable. There is clearly a coffee stain on it.

Japanese: 被害者は、たしかに飲んだのさ。
English: Conclusive proof that the victim did drink the poisoned coffee that was in this cup.

Japanese: 被告人が運んだコーヒーを‥‥ ‥‥こんなふうにッ！
English: The victim gulped down the bitter death that the waitress brought to him... like this!

Japanese: ううむむ‥‥ッ！
English: Uuuurgh!

Japanese: ちなみに、カップに残っていたのは 被害者と被告人の指紋だけッス。
English: For the record, the only prints on the cup were the victim's and the defendant's.

Japanese: 証拠品《コーヒーカップ》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Coffee Cup added to the Court Record.

Japanese: このカップからは‥‥ ある薬品が検出されたッス。
English: Upon further investigation of this cup... we found a certain chemical substance...

Japanese: （青酸カリ、か。名前ぐらい 聞いたことがあるな‥‥）
English: (Potassium cyanide? I've heard of that chemical before...)

Japanese: どのくらいのイキオイで 猛毒なんですか？
English: Exactly how strong is this poison, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥その。 もう、いわば、カーニバルッス！
English: It's... Well... That stuff's lethal!

Japanese: ヒトがたくさん死ぬッス！
English: Eat too much, and you're history.

Japanese: たくさん死ぬって。
English: How much is too much?

Japanese: 致死量は０．２グラム。 ‥‥それで、アンタはオシマイさ。
English: A lethal dose is 0.2 grams. That's about enough to finish anyone off.

Japanese: ０．２グラム、って‥‥ どれぐらいかな。
English: 0.2 grams? How much is that?

Japanese: 耳かきいっぱいの耳クソ ぐらいッス。
English: You know when you swab your ears for earwax? Yeah, about that much.

Japanese: （たっぷりありそうだな。 イトノコ刑事の耳クソ‥‥）
English: (Earwax, huh? Sounds like something Gumshoe's got an abundance of...)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。それぐらいなら どこにでも、かくせるでしょう。
English: Hmm... Such a small quantity of poison could have been concealed anywhere.

Japanese: “動機っぽい”‥‥？
English: "Some kind of a motive"?

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥個人的には、 ちっぽけなコトだと思うッスが‥‥
English: Yeah. But if you ask me, it's been blown way out of proportion.

Japanese: いいか、刑事。 ‥‥オレのルールでは‥‥
English: You know what my golden rule is, Detective?

Japanese: マズいコーヒーは捨てて、 すぐにおかわりをする。
English: Chuck out a bad cup of coffee. You can always get another.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうコトッスか？
English: Huh? I don't get it.

Japanese: アンタのおかわりなんぞ、 いくらでもいる、ってコトさ。
English: I'm saying, we can always get another witness on the stand, if we have to chuck you out.

Japanese: ‥‥むぐ。
English: *gulp*

Japanese: 証言するんだ。 被告人の“動機”を、な。
English: So stick to the facts, Detective. Now then, what was Ms. Byrde's motive?

Japanese: （‥‥イトノコ刑事‥‥）
English: (Come on, Gumshoe...)

Japanese: ど、動機は‥‥ 宝クジだと考えられているッス。
English: She was... They said she wanted to steal a lottery ticket.

Japanese: たからくじ‥‥ （きのうも聞いたハナシだな）
English: I knew it... (That's what we heard yesterday, too.)

Japanese: ‥‥事件の現場から、 消えちまったッス。
English: It disappeared from the scene of the crime.

Japanese: ただのクジじゃねえぜ。 ５０００万円の大当たりだ！
English: And it wasn't just any lottery ticket. It was a winning ticket for half a million!

Japanese: 本土坊さんも言ってたね。 クジのコト。
English: Mr. Armstrong knew about the ticket too, didn't he?

Japanese: まちがえて、となりの ハズレクジを盗んだ、って‥‥
English: But he stole the wrong one...

Japanese: （ホンモノは‥‥マコちゃんが 盗んだ、というコトか‥‥？）
English: (...Then is it possible Maggey stole the winning one?)

Japanese: （どうする‥‥？ もっとつっこんでみるか？）
English: (What should I do? Should I press on this point a little harder or not?)

Japanese: （いや。やめておこう。 イヤな予感がする‥‥）
English: (No, I should leave it alone. I've got a bad feeling about where this is going.)

Japanese: イヤな予感って、 たいてい当たるよねー。
English: If you've got a bad feeling about this, then maybe you should leave it alone.

Japanese: あたしなんか、イヤな予感が する予感がするコトがあるよ。
English: I once had a bad feeling that I was about to have a bad feeling. It was really bad.

Japanese: ‥‥とりあえず、他のコトを 聞いてみるよ。
English: ...Maybe I should move on to another part of his testimony.

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Wait a minute!

Japanese: クジが消えたからと言って、 被告人が盗んだコトにはならない！
English: The mere fact that the lottery ticket disappeared in no way implicates my client!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
ここに‥‥ そのクジがあるぜ。
English: I have here in my hand... the very ticket in question!

Japanese: それが‥‥ ご、ごせんまんえん‥‥
English: Th-That's the half a million dollar lottery ticket...?

Japanese: ‥‥現場で捜査中の 婦人警官が発見したらしいッス。
English: One of the female police officers found it when she was conducting a search...

Japanese: マコクン‥‥被告人の 身体検査をしているときに‥‥
English: ...of the defendant.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですってェェッ！
English: WH-WHAAAAAT!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ とんだラッキー・ガールだったぜ。
English: Ha...! She's quite a lucky bird, our little waitress.

Japanese: そ、その、そのクジを て‥‥提出してくださいッ！
English: Y-You will submit that ticket as evidence to the court! Immediately!

Japanese: （裁判長‥‥声が ウラがえってるぞ‥‥）
English: (Hmm, I'd better keep an eye on that ticket. The way the judge's voice is quivering...)

Japanese: 証拠品《宝クジ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Victim's Lottery Ticket added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 前回の法廷でも 目にしましたが‥‥
English: This ticket was presented to the court in the previous trial, too.

Japanese: お、重さがちがいますな。 ご、ごせんまんえん‥‥
English: But it feels heavier now somehow. H-Half a million dollars, you say...?

Japanese: ‥‥紙キレさ。
English: It's just a scrap of paper.

Japanese: 重要なのは‥‥その紙キレが、 どこから見つかったか、だぜ。
English: What matters is where it was found, Your Honor.

Japanese: （マコちゃんの身体、ってワケか）
English: (I bet he wants to say it was found on Maggey's person...)

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: やはり‥‥事件の真相は ハッキリしているようです。
English: The facts of this case seem overwhelmingly clear to me.

Japanese: 被告人には、犯行のチャンスが ありました。
English: The defendant had ample opportunity to commit the crime of which she is charged.

Japanese: そして、ジッサイにそれを行った 可能性も、きわめて高い。
English: Furthermore, it seems beyond reasonable doubt that she did indeed commit this crime.

Japanese: ものわかりのいいジイさん‥‥ キライじゃねえぜ。
English: I like an old man who knows the score.

Japanese: さらに、ご、５０００万円の 宝クジがあります。
English: There is also the matter of the half a million dollar lottery ticket.

Japanese: これはもう、動機として じゅうぶんです。
English: That alone provides a very credible motive.

Japanese: これだけのおカネ‥‥私ですら ちょっぴり、ココロがゆらぎます！
English: I mean, for that sum of money even I might be tempted to bend the rules!

Japanese: カネの魔力にヨワいジイさん‥‥ キライじゃねえぜ。
English: I don't mind an old man who is weak to the siren call of money.

Japanese: ううう‥‥ものすごい イキオイで証拠が出てきたね。
English: Not good, Nick... The evidence against Maggey is starting to pile up fast.

Japanese: 一度、有罪判決が 出ている裁判だからなあ‥‥
English: Yeah, that's because the court has ruled guilty once already.

Japanese: それじゃあ‥‥そろそろ、 この再放送を打ち切るぜ。
English: I'd say it's about time to wrap up this repeat performance...

Japanese: ‥‥決定的な証拠で、な。
English: ...with one final, decisive piece of evidence.

Japanese: （ま、まだあるのかよ！ 決定的な証拠‥‥）
English: (He's got more evidence against Maggey!?)

Japanese: コイツは、オレたちのマドンナが つけていた、エプロンだ。
English: This is the apron the delightful Ms. Byrde was wearing at the time.

Japanese: うわあ‥‥。ずいぶんキタナイ エプロンだね‥‥。
English: Wow... That's not the cleanest apron I've seen...

Japanese: あの‥‥こ、このシミは‥‥？ （まさか‥‥“血”か‥‥！）
English: Th-That stain looks like...! (It can't be... blood, can it!?)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　あわてんぼうの ウエイトレスさんだぜ‥‥。
English: Ha...! It seems the star of our play was a little flustered...

Japanese: どうやら‥‥、コーヒーを ひっくり返したらしいな。
English: And somehow spilled coffee on herself.

Japanese: こ、コーヒー？ いやいや。そうじゃなくて‥‥
English: The coffee!? That's not exactly the first thing that caught my eye...

Japanese: そう。‥‥モンダイは、 コーヒーのシミなんかじゃねえ。
English: Of course. The coffee stain isn't the most interesting thing about this apron.

Japanese: このエプロンについている、 もう１つのシロモノだ。
English: No, there's something else that stands out even more.

Japanese: もう１つの‥‥ そ、それは、当然‥‥
English: Something else? I presume you mean...

Japanese: モチロン、ポケットさ。
English: Of course. I'm referring to the pocket.

Japanese: ぽ、ポケット‥‥？
English: The... pocket?

Japanese: 事件発生後、身体検査の際‥‥ ポケットから、出てきやがった。
English: A search carried out right after the incident uncovered this...

Japanese: 青酸カリだ。 殺害に使用された、猛毒さ‥‥。
English: Potassium cyanide. The very poison used by the killer, was in her apron pocket!

Japanese: ど、毒薬のビンが‥‥ 被告人のエプロンから？
English: A bottle of poison... In Maggey's pocket?

Japanese: ‥‥被告人の指紋だけが 付着しているッス。
English: Yeah. And Maggey's prints were the only ones on it.

Japanese: なんですってえええェェッ！
English: WHAAAT!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ とにかく、証拠品を受理します！
English: Order! Order! Order! The court will accept these items into evidence.

Japanese: 証拠品《エプロン》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Apron added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 証拠品《青酸カリ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Potassium Cyanide added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ところで‥‥さっきから フシギだったのですが‥‥
English: There's something still bothering me, Mr. Godot.

Japanese: なぜ‥‥この血痕について、 セツメイがないのですかな？
English: Why have you not explained the bloodstain to the court?

Japanese: 《血痕》だと‥‥？ なんのコトだ、いったい。
English: Bloodstain? What bloodstain would that be?

Japanese: いやいや！　まっ赤な血が、 このようにドバッとベッタリ‥‥
English: Don't play games, prosecutor! The blood-colored stain that's smeared all over the apron!

Japanese: ば‥‥バカな！ 聞いてねえぞ、オレは！
English: That's ridiculous! No one told me anything about a bloodstain!

Japanese: （“聞いてない”もナニも、 見ればわかるじゃないか！）
English: (You don't need to be told! Just look at it!)

Japanese: どうなんだい、刑事‥‥！
English: Well, Detective!?

Japanese: まさか、コイツは‥‥ 血ィを吸ってると言うのかッ！
English: Could this stain really be... BLOOD!?

Japanese: い‥‥いや、そのッ！
English: N-No way, sir!

Japanese: それは‥‥いわゆる、あの。 ケチャップッス。
English: That's... It's just ketchup. Sir.

Japanese: け、けちゃっぷ‥‥？
English: Ketchup!?

Japanese: この日のモーニングセットを運ぶ とき、ぶちまけたらしいッス。
English: She must've gotten some on her apron while taking someone their breakfast that day.

Japanese: そういうコトは、 早く言いなさいッ！
English: You could have spoken up a little sooner, Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: 今度やったら、ケチャップを １７杯、飲んでもらうぜ！
English: Pull a stunt like that again, and I'll have you drink 17 cups of ketchup, witness!

Japanese: うううう‥‥いくらなんでも 気がついてると思ったッス‥‥
English: Unnngh... I thought everyone knew what it was already.

Japanese: ‥‥やはり、前回の有罪判決は マチガイではなかったようです。
English: Hmm... I haven't seen anything yet to make me doubt the last verdict I ruled on this case.

Japanese: 決定的な証拠‥‥動機、 そして、犯行のチャンス。
English: The motive, the opportunity, and the supporting evidence...

Japanese: ‥‥すべてが立証されました。
English: They have all been clearly established.

Japanese: どうやら、まるほどう‥‥
English: Well, Trite...

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥アンタは ニセモノだったみたいだぜ。
English: It seems you really are a phony after all.

Japanese: （く、くそお‥‥）
English: (Argh! You really know how to drive a man nuts!)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、証人。 証言をつづけていただきます。
English: Witness, please continue with your testimony.

Japanese: 事件の発見、そして‥‥ その後の捜査について！
English: Describe for the court the crime scene and the findings of your investigation there.

Japanese: ～通報から捜査まで～
English: -- The Investigation --

Japanese: 通報は、おっかないジイさんから、 ２時２５分に入ったッス。
English: The crime was reported at 2:25 PM by a kind of scary old man, sir.

Japanese: カワイソウに、マコクンは ショックでキゼツしていたッス。
English: Poor Maggey had passed out from the shock. It must've been real tough for her.

Japanese: 被害者は、身分証明書などを 身につけていなかったッス。
English: The victim didn't have any identification on him.

Japanese: さいわい、すぐに身元がわかった ので、捜査は順調だったッスが。
English: But we figured out who he was pretty quick, and then the investigation went smoothly.

Japanese: マコクンの身体検査で、宝クジと 毒薬の小ビンが発見されたッス。
English: When Maggey was searched, we found the lottery ticket and the bottle of poison.

Japanese: 他に現場からなくなったものは、 いっさいなかったッス。
English: And that was it. There was nothing else missing from the crime scene.

Japanese: 身元の特定、容疑者のカクホ‥‥ 初動捜査にも、問題ありませんな。
English: Hmm. You identified the victim and secured your prime suspect... Very good.

Japanese: ホンモノとニセモノのちがいを 見せる、最後のチャンスだな。
English: Last chance to convince the court you're a real lawyer, Trite!

Japanese: 最後の尋問になるかもしれねえ。 楽しませてくれよ、弁護士クン‥‥
English: Don't count on any more cross-examinations after this one! So, let the fun begin!

Japanese: ～通報から捜査まで～
English: -- The Investigation --

Japanese: 通報は、おっかないジイさんから、 ２時２５分に入ったッス。
English: The crime was reported at 2:25 PM by a kind of scary old man, sir.

Japanese: カワイソウに、マコクンは ショックでキゼツしていたッス。
English: Poor Maggey had passed out from the shock. It must've been real tough for her.

Japanese: 被害者は、身分証明書などを 身につけていなかったッス。
English: The victim didn't have any identification on him.

Japanese: さいわい、すぐに身元がわかった ので、捜査は順調だったッスが。
English: But we figured out who he was pretty quick, and then the investigation went smoothly.

Japanese: マコクンの身体検査で、宝クジと 毒薬の小ビンが発見されたッス。
English: When Maggey was searched, we found the lottery ticket and the bottle of poison.

Japanese: 他に現場からなくなったものは、 いっさいなかったッス。
English: And that was it. There was nothing else missing from the crime scene.

Japanese: （この証言がくずせなければ、 ほぼ判決はキマリだ‥‥！）
English: If I don't find a hole in this testimony, the judge is going to hand down his verdict!

Japanese: でも‥‥イトノコさん、 ウソは言ってないみたいだよ。
English: Gumshoe isn't giving us anything to work with...

Japanese: これじゃあ、ムジュンしようが ないじゃない！
English: And we can't find any contradictions if he doesn't give us something!

Japanese: たしかに、それはそうだけど‥‥
English: Yeah, that's true...

Japanese: イトノコ刑事とマコちゃんは、 無類のウッカリコンビだ。
English: But Maggey and Gumshoe are like dumb and dumber.

Japanese: 何かを見落としてるかもしれない。 ‥‥その可能性を信じよう。
English: Our only hope is that they were so dumb, they missed something obvious.

Japanese: ‥‥おねがい！　いつも以上に ウッカリしてて！　イトノコさん！
English: Come on, Gumshoe! Be the dumbest you've ever been!

Japanese: “ジイさん”というのは‥‥？
English: "Scary old man", Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: なんでも、事件があったレストラン の常連らしいッス。
English: There's an old man who's a regular at the restaurant where the incident happened.

Japanese: （あのジイさんだよな、 やっぱり‥‥）
English: (Ah, we're obviously talking about the same old man...)

Japanese: 通報を受けて、警官が急いで トンで行ったッスが‥‥
English: Officers were dispatched right after the report came in, but the old guy still made a fuss.

Japanese: “遅いワ！”とドヤしつけられて、 コゴトとマメを食らったッス。
English: "What took you so long!?" Then he hurled abuse at them. And seeds...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥マメを。
English: Hmm... Seeds...

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ オレは、歯で受けとめたがな。
English: Ha...! It was nothing! I caught each one with my teeth.

Japanese: （ゴドー検事も食らったのか‥‥）
English: (I guess not even the mighty Godot can avoid being attacked by that guy...)

Japanese: 事件当時、店の客は その老人だけだった。
English: The old man was the only other customer in the place at the time.

Japanese: もたもた公衆電話を探して、 通報が遅れたらしいぜ。
English: He took his time finding a pay phone apparently, so he was late reporting the crime.

Japanese: 被告人は、どれぐらい 意識を失っていたんですか？
English: How long was the defendant unconscious for?

Japanese: 警官たちが現場についたのが、 ２時４０分ごろ‥‥。
English: The officers got to the crime scene at around 2:40.

Japanese: マコクンは、キッチンで すやすや寝ていたらしいッス。
English: Maggey was still out cold in the kitchen at that time.

Japanese: 目がさめるまで‥‥ かれこれ１０分はかかったッス。
English: It took another 10 minutes or so before she came to.

Japanese: 自分も、現場にいたかったッス。
English: I woulda liked to have been on the scene myself.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥ 捜査したかっただろうな）
English: (I bet you would've liked to have carried out the search, too...)

Japanese: 自分は、その。マコクンの 寝ガオに見とれていたかったッス。
English: I woulda loved to see Maggey asleep like that, all pretty and peaceful.

Japanese: （‥‥シゴトしろよ！）
English: (You're a professional detective, Gumshoe. Not a professional bird watcher!)

Japanese: ロマンティックな世界は、 アンタだけのモノさ。
English: Save the romantics for your own time, Detective.

Japanese: オレたちが知りたいのは‥‥ 捜査のコト、だぜ。
English: All we need to know about is the investigation.

Japanese: ちょっぴり、赤面しちゃったッス。
English: Oops! I guess I'm pretty red right now, aren't I?

Japanese: ‥‥それは、“盗まれた” というイミですか？
English: He didn't have any? Are you saying that it was stolen, then?

Japanese: そういうワケでもないッス。
English: No, I don't think so.

Japanese: 被害者の岡　高夫は、免許証も パスポートも持っておらず‥‥
English: The victim didn't have a driver's license or even a credit card on him, pal.

Japanese: サイフには５８円しか 入っていなかったッス。
English: All he had was 58 cents in his wallet.

Japanese: ご、ごじゅうはちえん‥‥
English: Fifty-eight cents...?

Japanese: ‥‥わずかながら、 自分が勝ったッス。
English: Yeah. I can't believe I found someone with less in their wallet than me, pal!

Japanese: なげかわしいワカモノですなあ。
English: The victim sounds like he was a thoroughly miserable young man.

Japanese: “アウトロー”気取り‥‥ それもまた“ワカさ”ってヤツさ。
English: Or some kind of outlaw... Why not give him a bit of an edge?

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、引っかかるな‥‥）
English: (I think I'm on to something here...)

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Wait a sec!

Japanese: おッ！　またバカなコト 言っちまったッスか？　自分は。
English: Huh? D-Did I say something dumb again!?

Japanese: ‥‥証人は、ついさっき、 こう証言していますよね？
English: Let me paraphrase what you just testified to this court.

Japanese: “被害者は、身分証明書を 持っていなかった”‥‥と。
English: "The victim didn't have any form of ID on him." That's basically what you said, yes?

Japanese: 言っちまったッス。
English: Yeah, basically.

Japanese: それならば‥‥どうして、すぐに 被害者の身元がわかったんですか？
English: In that case, how were you able to identify the victim so quickly?

Japanese: なんだ。そんなコトッスか。
English: Oh... That.

Japanese: （ロコツにガッカリされた‥‥）
English: (He's so let down, he's got the whole sagging shoulders and puppy eyes thing going...)

Japanese: 被害者のテーブルの上には、 宝クジと‥‥薬袋があったッス。
English: There was a prescription bag on the victim's table along with the lottery ticket.

Japanese: 岡　高夫は、吐麗美庵に現れる前に ‥‥医者に行っていたらしいぜ。
English: It seems Mr. Glen Elg visited his doctor before he went to Trés Bien.

Japanese: クスリを処方した医者のカルテから 被害者の名前がわかったッス。
English: We got the victim's name from the medical records of the doc who prescribed the meds.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 モンダイないようですな。
English: Hmm... That's a reliable enough source for the court.

Japanese: （どうしよう‥‥ もっと詳しく話を聞くか‥‥？）
English: (What should I do? Should I leave this alone or ask to hear more?)

Japanese: （被害者の身元を割り出した 方法なんか、どうでもいい‥‥）
English: (I guess it doesn't really matter how they figured out who the victim was.)

Japanese: （もっと、重要な 手がかりを探すんだ‥‥！）
English: (There are more important avenues to explore.)

Japanese: その薬袋には、なんの クスリが入っていたんですか？
English: So what sort of medicine was in the bag?

Japanese: それが‥‥‥
English: Well...

Japanese: 薬袋の中には、 何も入っていなかったッス。
English: Actually, the bag we found was empty.
Huh?

Japanese: カラッポだったッス。
English: Yeah. Completely empty.

Japanese: （カラッポの薬袋、か‥‥）
English: (It was completely empty...?)

Japanese: 証拠品《被害者の薬袋》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Victim's Prescription Bag added to the Court Record.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
カラッポの手がかりをもらって マンゾクしたかい‥‥？
English: You're entering an empty paper bag as evidence? Desperate, are you, Trite?

Japanese: それで‥‥その後、 捜査はどうなったんだ？　刑事。
English: Now, what happened with the investigation after that, Detective?

Japanese: 被害者は、身分証明書を 持っていなかったんですよね？
English: So, the victim had no form of identification on him, correct?

Japanese: そッス。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: しかし‥‥彼は、現場に来る前に、 医者に行っている！
English: And yet, before arriving at the restaurant, he went to see a doctor.

Japanese: それならば‥‥
English: Which means...

Japanese: 保険証を持っているハズ じゃないですか？
English: He must've had his medical insurance card with him!

Japanese: あ‥‥それはそうですね！
English: Oh! That is very true!

Japanese: それが見あたらない ということは‥‥
English: The fact that it hasn't been found points to only one obvious conclusion.

Japanese: 被害者の保険証は、 何者かに盗まれたコトになる！
English: The victim's medical card was stolen!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
たしかに、被害者は 保険証を持っていなかった。
English: True, the victim didn't have a medical insurance card.

Japanese: なんせ‥‥そもそも、岡　高夫は 保険に入っていなかったからな。
English: But why? Because Mr. Glen Elg didn't have medical insurance to begin with!
Huh?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。ホントに、 なげかわしいワカモノですなあ。
English: Hmm... As I thought, a thoroughly miserable young man.

Japanese: “アウトロー”気取り‥‥ それもまた“ワカさ”ってヤツさ。
English: Or some kind of outlaw... A bit of an edge never hurt anyone.

Japanese: なんだか‥‥ ハズレ、みたいなフンイキだね。
English: Looks like we're off course again, Nick.

Japanese: それでは、証言を つづけていただきましょう。
English: Witness. Please continue with your testimony.

Japanese: その後の捜査について、 おねがいします。
English: Explain for the court the events following the preliminary investigation.

Japanese: しかし‥‥被告人は キゼツしていたんですよね‥‥？
English: But the defendant had been passed out for a while, correct?

Japanese: それならば‥‥
English: In that case...

Japanese: ダレかが、被告人のポケットに 証拠品を入れたのかもしれません！
English: Isn't it possible someone planted the evidence in Maggey's pocket!?

Japanese: おおッ！　そ、それッス！ いただきッス！
English: Hey! Y-Yeah! You've nailed it, pal!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 自分も、そういうコトが よくあるッス！
English: It happens to me all the time!

Japanese: こないだも、忘年会で盛り上がって ウチに帰ってきたら‥‥
English: We had a department party the other day, and when I got home...

Japanese: なぜか、カチョーのクツを はいていたッス！
English: ...I was wearing the boss's shoes!

Japanese: くだらねえコトを言ってると‥‥ カチョーのクツ、食わせるぜ！
English: Keep up this crazy testimony, Detective, and those shoes will end up down your throat!
Sorry...

Japanese: “ダレかが、被告人のポケットに 証拠品を入れた”‥‥？
English: So, Trite. "Someone planted the evidence in Maggey's pocket"...?

Japanese: ゴタクはいらねえ。 それを立証する証拠を出しなッ！
English: That's a pretty bold statement. Care to back it up with some evidence?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥ （あれば、出してるよ‥‥）
English: Um... Well... (I'd love to, if I had any...)

Japanese: ‥‥証拠は、ないようですね。
English: It appears you have no evidence to support your theory, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: それでは、証言をつづけて いただきましょうか。
English: Continue with your testimony, witness.

Japanese: ５０００万円の当たりクジと、 毒薬の小ビン、ですか‥‥？
English: So the half a million dollar lottery ticket and the bottle of poison were accounted for.

Japanese: そッス。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: おかしいですね‥‥
English: Interesting... It's true that those two items are accounted for...

Japanese: たしか‥‥もう１枚、 宝クジが盗まれたハズでは？
English: ...but wasn't there another lottery ticket that was stolen that day?

Japanese: ああ。店長が盗んだ、アレッスか。
English: Oh, yeah. The one the restaurant owner took.

Japanese: １００円、当たったらしいッス。 ‥‥ラッキーなオトコッスね。
English: He won a whole dollar with it. What a lucky guy, huh?

Japanese: （どうやら、おとがめはナシ、 みたいだな‥‥）
English: (And they're just going to let him get away with it...?)

Japanese: まあ、１００円ぽっちではねえ。
English: It was just one dollar, Detective.

Japanese: 金額のモンダイなのかなあ‥‥
English: I guess no one cares when it's that little... Unless it's Gumshoe.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事。お望みどおり ‥‥たたきつけてあげましょう。
English: Detective Gumshoe. I think I should point something out to you.

Japanese: ちょっとしたムジュンを。
English: There is just one small contradiction in your testimony.

Japanese: お！　ついに来たッス！
English: Oho! Finally!

Japanese: ちょっぴり、 ムネがワクワクするッスね‥‥
English: I'm getting all anxious just waiting, so hurry up, will you?

Japanese: 『現場から消えたモノはない』 ‥‥あなたは、そう証言しました。
English: You testified that, "nothing else was missing from the crime scene."

Japanese: しかし！　被害者の薬袋は カラッポだったそうですね！
English: However! The prescription bag you mentioned was empty!

Japanese: 現場から、被害者のクスリは 見つかったんですか？
English: Did the officers recover the medicine from the scene of the crime later?

Japanese: い、いや！ それはなかったッス‥‥
English: Um, no. They didn't.

Japanese: 被害者は、現場に来る前に 医者からクスリをもらっている。
English: The victim was given a prescription right before going to Trés Bien.

Japanese: 薬袋がカラッポのはずが ありませんッ！
English: Where, then, did the medicine disappear to!?
Y-You...

Japanese: カッコイイッスううううゥゥゥッ！
English: ...are toooooo cool, pal!!

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに！
English: I-Indeed!

Japanese: 前回の裁判では、被害者のクスリ など、審議されませんでした！
English: Due consideration wasn't given to the victim's prescription in the previous trial.

Japanese: 証人！　あなたはなぜ、 いつもこうなのですかッ！
English: Witness! Why do you always overlook such vital pieces of evidence!?

Japanese: い‥‥いつも以上に ウッカリしてたッスゥゥゥッ！
English: I, er... I guess that's the most careless thing I've done so far, huh?

Japanese: 被害者は、“毒薬”によって 殺害された！
English: The victim was killed by poison!

Japanese: そして‥‥被害者の“クスリ”が 消えてしまっている‥‥
English: And the victim's "medicine" mysteriously disappeared!

Japanese: その消えたクスリが、 毒薬だったのかもしれません！
English: The victim's own prescription could have been the lethal poison itself!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！
English: O-Order! Order!

Japanese: いかがですか！　ゴドー検事ッ！
English: Well, Mr. Godot? What do you have to say to that?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ とだけ、言っておこうか。
English: Ha...! That's all.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか！
English: What!?

Japanese: 薬袋に書かれている 医者の名前‥‥よく読んでみな。
English: Read for the court the name of the clinic on the prescription bag, if you will.

Japanese: 医者の‥‥名前‥‥？
English: What's the clinic's name got to do with anything...?

Japanese: 《ニューイヤー耳鼻科クリニック》 ‥‥じ、じびか‥‥？
English: "New Ear Otolaryngological Clinic" O-Otolaryngological?

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥被害者は、 いったい、なんの病気で‥‥？
English: Just what kind of illness was the victim suffering from, Mr. Godot?

Japanese: 病気‥‥というのはちがうぜ。 ニガい戦利品、てヤツだな。
English: Hardly an illness, Your Honor. More like a bitter war wound, you could say.

Japanese: せんりひん‥‥？
English: A war wound?

Japanese: 岡　高夫は、事件の前日 だれかとケンカしたらしい。
English: The day before the incident, Mr. Elg found himself in a fight.

Japanese: その際、横っツラをビンタされて、 コマクが破れちまったのさ。
English: He took a blow to the side of the head, and ruptured his eardrum.

Japanese: ‥‥こ、コマク‥‥
English: He ruptured his eardrum!?

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥処方された “クスリ”というのは？
English: Then what on earth was the prescription he was given?

Japanese: 耳の中にたらす、外用薬さ。 ‥‥飲み薬じゃァねえぜ！
English: It was a cream that was to be applied topically inside his ear canal, not to be ingested!

Japanese: な‥‥‥なんですってぇぇぇッ！
English: What!?

Japanese: 解剖記録の所見に、 小さく書いてあるぜ。
English: It's mentioned in the autopsy report, if you read the fine print.

Japanese: 被害者の左耳から そのクスリが検出された、ってな。
English: They found traces of the medication in the victim's left ear.

Japanese: あ。たしかに‥‥これまた ちっちゃく書いてありますな‥‥。
English: Yes. Here it is. In very, very fine print.

Japanese: どうやら岡　高夫は、吐麗美庵で そのクスリを使用したらしい。
English: It seems Mr. Elg correctly applied some of his medication while he was at Trés Bien.

Japanese: ということは、まちがって飲んだ ってのも、あり得ねえ。
English: Therefore, it would be absurd to believe that he would have eaten his medication.

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Uuurgh...

Japanese: どうやら‥‥このクスリは 事件とは無関係、のようですな。
English: It seems that this medication is irrelevant to the case after all.

Japanese: は‥‥はあ‥‥
English: N-No...

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: ここで認めちゃったら 終わっちゃうよ！　裁判！
English: If you don't think of something quick, it'll all be over!

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥！）
English: (She's right.)

Japanese: （ヘタな異議はユルされない！ どうする‥‥？）
English: (But I can't get away with any old weak objection. What should I do...?)

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり‥‥クスリは 事件と無関係、みたいです。
English: It does seem as if the medication isn't connected to the case.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
ムダな時間を使わなかったぶん、 前回の弁護士のほうがマシだぜ。
English: Maybe the last Mr. Trite was the real one. At least he didn't waste time.

Japanese: そうですね‥‥
English: Indeed...

Japanese: クスリについて、これ以上 追求がないのであれば‥‥
English: If you have nothing further to add to this medication issue...

Japanese: 前回の法廷と、状況は まったく変わりません。
English: ...the facts of this case seem no different to those presented in the last trial.

Japanese: ‥‥つまり‥‥判決を 下すしかない、ということですね。
English: I would therefore have no choice but to hand down my verdict.

Japanese: え‥‥‥ええええッ！
English: Uh... Umm...!

Japanese: だ、ダメだよ！　やっぱり、 ここは食い下がらないと！
English: Nick! Don't let him do this! You've got to stand your ground!

Japanese: それでは‥‥
English: Now then...

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長！
English: Wait, Your Honor!

Japanese: このクスリは‥‥じつは、事件と 深ぁい関係がありますッ！
English: Actually, this prescription... is absolutely pivotal to the case!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人‥‥
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: さっきと言ってるコトが ゼンゼンちがうではないですかッ！
English: Did you not just say the exact opposite only moments ago!?

Japanese: （‥‥い、今はガマンだ‥‥）
English: (Gotta lose a few battles in order to win the war, I suppose.)

Japanese: さきほど、ゴドー検事は こう発言しました！
English: Only moments ago, Mr. Godot made the following statement.

Japanese: 『岡　高夫は、吐麗美庵で あのクスリを使用した』‥‥
English: "It seems Mr. Elg correctly applied some of his medication while he was at Trés Bien."

Japanese: ‥‥それならば、なぜ！ クスリは消えたのか！
English: If that's the case, then why was the medication not found at the scene of the crime!?

Japanese: しかし‥‥そのクスリは、 耳に使う、外用薬ですぞ！
English: But the medication in question was for topical use inside the ear canal!

Japanese: 現場から消えたという ジジツは変わりません！
English: That doesn't change the fact that it could not be found at the crime scene!

Japanese: 少しでもギモンが残るかぎり‥‥ 弁護側は審理を求めます！
English: However insignificant it may seem, it's a lawyer's duty to pursue the truth!

Japanese: そんなクスリにイミがないコトは、 アンタもわかってるだろうぜ。
English: You know as well as I do that the medication is irrelevant.

Japanese: 医者が出したクスリに、 青酸カリが入っているハズがねえ！
English: It hardly seems likely that a prescription drug would contain potassium cyanide.

Japanese: ウエイトレスが運んだコーヒーにも 青酸カリが入っているハズはない！
English: It hardly seems likely that the coffee the waitress served would contain it, either!

Japanese: でも、ジッサイには入っていた！ 可能性は変わりません！
English: But it did! The possibility is undeniable!

Japanese: ‥‥ぐううぅぅ‥‥
English: Aaargh!

Japanese: ‥‥もう、けっこうです。
English: That's enough.

Japanese: ゴドー検事。検察側が用意した 証人は、この刑事だけなのですか？
English: Mr. Godot. Is the detective the only witness the prosecution wishes to call?

Japanese: ‥‥検事さん？
English: Mr. Godot...?

Japanese: あ。あの。自分が今朝 つれてきた証人がいるッス。
English: Um... I, ah... I've got my own witness I'd like to call, sir.

Japanese: 事件当日、レストランにいた おジイさんッスけど。
English: It's the old man who was there in the restaurant on the day of the murder.

Japanese: （五十嵐　将兵、か。 ハトにエサをやっていた‥‥）
English: (Victor Kudo? The pigeon-hater?)

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: 小さなクスリが消えたのは、 ササイなことです。
English: The matter of the disappearing medication seems little more than trivial at best.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。
English: However!

Japanese: 前回の裁判で、その問題は いっさい触れられなかった。
English: It wasn't explored at all in the previous trial.

Japanese: それが私には気になります。
English: And that is something that bothers me.

Japanese: や‥‥やったよ！　なるほどくん。
English: Yay! Good job, Nick!

Japanese: ここで、いったん １０分間の休憩をとります。
English: The court will adjourn for a 10 minute recess.

Japanese: 検察側は、新しい証人の 準備をしておくように。
English: After which we will hear the prosecution's next witness.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ どうやら‥‥
残りの６杯で、決着をつけるしか ねえようだな‥‥。
English: I suppose this means I'll just have to finish you off in my last 6 cups.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、休憩に入ります！
English: Court is adjourned for recess!

Japanese: 同日　午前１１時　３分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: January 7, 11:03 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: い、いやあ‥‥ あぶなかったねえ。
English: Phew! That was close!

Japanese: ‥‥ううう‥‥死ぬかと思った。
English: Tell me about it... I nearly died in there!

Japanese: それはこっちのセリフッス！
English: That's my line, sir!

Japanese: あたしのセリフだよ！ ジッサイ、ちょっと死んでたかも。
English: No, it's my line! I think I really did die a little bit.

Japanese: ヘタしたら、ここにいる３人 みんな死んでたみたいッスね‥‥
English: Looks like we all nearly died in there...

Japanese: イトノコセンパイ、ヒドいッス！ ウラ切り者ッス！
English: I can't believe Detective Gumshoe! How could he betray us like that!?
Huh?

Japanese: アタシを助けてくれる、 って言ってたのに‥‥
English: He said he'd help me.

Japanese: 思いっきり、 クビをしめられたッス！
English: But he totally set me up!

Japanese: あ‥‥。でも、 しかたないんじゃないかな。
English: I don't think he meant to do that, Maggey. He was backed into a corner.

Japanese: イトノコさんも、ホラ。 シゴトだし‥‥
English: I mean, the guy's got to do his job, right?

Japanese: スズキ、ウソとウラ切りには 慣れてるッス。　‥‥でも！
English: It's OK. I know all about lies and betrayal. I've had them my whole life.

Japanese: 今回のは、ハラにイッパツ、 ズンと来たッス！
English: But it really hurt this time. It felt like someone punched me hard in the stomach!

Japanese: もー、キライッス！ カオも見たくないッス！
English: I hate that guy! I don't ever want to see him again!

Japanese: （かわいそうなイトノコ刑事‥‥）
English: (Poor Gumshoe...)

Japanese: 次の証人‥‥って、 あのオジさんだよね。
English: So the next witness is going to be that old guy from the park, right?

Japanese: ああ、イガラシさんね。 制服とマメが大好きな。
English: Yeah. Mr. Kudo. Lover of waitress outfits and projectile seeds!

Japanese: ‥‥きっと、手ゴワいと思うッス。 シッカリしたオジさんッスから。
English: I bet he's gonna be really stubborn. I mean, he's pretty set in his ways, you know?

Japanese: ガンコそうだしねえ‥‥。 だいじょうぶ？　なるほどくん。
English: Yeah, he's a big old grouch. Are you going to be able to handle him, Nick?

Japanese: ああ。しっかり受けとめてやるさ。 ‥‥あのマメを、この顔面で！
English: Yeah, I can take whatever he throws at me. Even those never-ending bird seeds!

Japanese: 同日　午前１１時１５分 地方裁判所　第４法廷
English: January 7, 11:15 AM District Court Courtroom No. 4

Japanese: それでは、審理を再開しましょう。
English: Court will now reconvene for the trial of Maggey Byrde.

Japanese: ゴドー検事。 次の証人をおねがいします。
English: Mr. Godot. Your next witness, please.

Japanese: コーヒーを飲みながら、ショーを 楽しんだ、ラッキーな老人‥‥
English: The prosecution calls the lucky old-timer who caught the show over a cup of coffee.

Japanese: 入廷させてやってくれねえか。
English: Will the witness please take the stand.

Japanese: ‥‥ナニモノだ？　アンタ。
English: Name and occupation, if you don't mind.

Japanese: 手前、五十嵐　将兵と申すもの。 生まれも育ちも下町でございます。
English: The name is Victor Kudo. Born and bred in the Land of the Rising Sun!

Japanese: ギリと人情にゃチト、アツい。 けれどもちょっぴりナミダもろい。
English: Honor and duty are what make me. Mind you, I can be quite emotional at times, too.

Japanese: い、いやいや。それはいいから 職業を聞かせてください。
English: We don't need to hear about that, Mr. Kudo. Just tell the court your occupation.

Japanese: シゴト‥‥
English: My occupation?

Japanese: いいか、若いの。この不景気で 紋つきが売れると思うかッ！
English: Kah! Listen, young'un! How much call d'you think there is for kimono embroidery here!?

Japanese: もんつき‥‥？
English: Kimono embroidery?

Japanese: ワシは職人なのだ！ 和服に、家紋を描いておる。
English: That's what I do! Or did, back in Japan. I embroidered family crests on kimonos!

Japanese: 紋章上絵師という、下町ジョウチョ あふれる家業をいとなんでおる！
English: My ancestors were embroidering kimonos before this country even existed!

Japanese: うわあ。職人さんだよ！ 下町ッ子だねえ。
English: Wow, a real craftsman! They're a dying breed!

Japanese: いっちょ、あたしも 描いてもらおうかなあ。
English: Hey! Maybe he could embroider my costume sometime.

Japanese: ま。本職がサッパリなのでな。
English: Anyway, like I said. There's not much demand for that kind of thing here.

Japanese: 最近はハンバーガー屋のレジで、 ムリヤリ笑顔をうかべておる始末。
English: So I had to take a job working the cash register at a burger joint... pretending to smile!

Japanese: （キッチンに引っこんでろよ‥‥）
English: (That burger joint would've been better off putting him in the kitchen...)

Japanese: 証人。あなたは事件当日、 現場のレストランにいましたね？
English: Now then, witness. Were you in the restaurant at the time of the incident?

Japanese: さよう。マメ食って、 コーシー飲んでましたな。
English: Oh, yes! I was eating some seeds over a cup of javaccino.

Japanese: まめ？
English: Seeds?

Japanese: コイツですわいッ！
English: What do you think these are, hmm!?

Japanese: はああ。
English: I-I see...

Japanese: ‥‥ジイさん。アンタ、 見たんだろう？　あの日。
English: So you saw everything that happened, gramps?

Japanese: よくぞ聞いてくださった！ そのとおり！　ワシは見た！
English: Did I!? Oh, yes! Oh, yes I did! I saw it all!

Japanese: そいつを話してみな。 ‥‥聞いてやるぜ。
English: Then please, tell the court. We're all ears.

Japanese: あらよッと！　どうか、 ココロして聞いてもらいたいッ！
English: Sure, sure. I'll tell you. I'll tell you every last detail!

Japanese: （元気なジイさんだな‥‥）
English: (He's really getting into this...)

Japanese: ～目撃したこと～
English: -- What I Witnessed --

Japanese: その客は、 スポーツ新聞を読んでおった。
English: The young man was reading the sports paper.

Japanese: 女給さんがコーシーを運んできた のだが‥‥中に、何か入れたのだ！
English: The serving girl brought him a javaccino... But she put something in it!

Japanese: 客が、そのコーシーを飲んだ瞬間！ いきなり苦しみ、そして倒れたッ！
English: The man took one sip of it, looked like he was in terrible pain, and then collapsed.

Japanese: 女給さんの正体こそ、被告席の 婦女子。よぉく、おぼえておる！
English: That's the serving girl, right there in the defendant's chair. I remember her well!

Japanese: “女給”‥‥は、よくありません。 ウエイトレスさん、ですな。
English: Mr. Kudo, she is not a "serving girl". Please refer to her as a waitress.

Japanese: かぁッ！　なげかわしい！
English: Kah! You're as bad as the rest of them!

Japanese: アンタみたいなジイさんまで、 西洋かぶれかッ！
English: All these newfangled words! What's wrong with old-fashioned ones, hmm!?

Japanese: せ‥‥西洋‥‥
English: N-Newfangled?

Japanese: ワシに言わせれば、トイレは便所！ ティッシュはあくまで、ちり紙！
English: All this talk of "radios" and "glasses"! It's "wireless" and "spectacles", I tell you!

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。
English: E-Excuse me...

Japanese: みなみなサマがた！
English: Listen to me, everyone!

Japanese: もっと、自分の国のコトバを タイセツにせにゃイカンですぞ！
English: Don't forget the old values! Don't let the good old days slip away!

Japanese: ‥‥では、そういうことで 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Well, um... I think it's time to begin the cross- examination, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ～目撃したこと～
English: -- What I Witnessed --

Japanese: その客は、 スポーツ新聞を読んでおった。
English: The young man was reading the sports paper.

Japanese: 女給さんがコーシーを運んできた のだが‥‥中に、何か入れたのだ！
English: The serving girl brought him a javaccino... But she put something in it!

Japanese: 客が、そのコーシーを飲んだ瞬間！ いきなり苦しみ、そして倒れたッ！
English: The man took one sip of it, looked like he was in terrible pain, and then collapsed.

Japanese: 女給さんの正体こそ、被告席の 婦女子。よぉく、おぼえておる！
English: That's the serving girl, right there in the defendant's chair. I remember her well!

Japanese: マメのオジさん‥‥ マコちゃんを目撃してるのかな。
English: Do you think Old Seedy really saw Maggey do it?

Japanese: そりゃあ、ウエイトレスさんを 見に行ってるワケだからなあ。
English: Well, he probably had his eye on the waitress the whole time. That's why he was there.

Japanese: でも。ウエイトレスさんの 制服が目当てなんでしょ？
English: But he was there for the cute outfits, right? Not the waitress...

Japanese: あ‥‥ （そういえば、そうだったな‥‥）
English: I-I guess... (Hmm, she makes a good point though...)

Japanese: お！　あたし今、 いいコト言ったんじゃない？
English: Hey! Did I just say something clever?

Japanese: 五十嵐さんが見たウエイトレスが ホントにマコちゃんだったか‥‥
English: I wonder if the waitress Mr. Kudo saw really was Maggey...

Japanese: たしかめる必要があるよね！
English: That's what we have to figure out, Nick!

Japanese: まちがいない！　ワシに見せつける かのように、白いコナをッ！
English: There's no question about it! She very conspicuously put some white powder in there!

Japanese: 髪をゆわいたリボン。それに、 前かけのヒモが、ほどけておった。
English: There was a ribbon in her hair, and her apron's straps were loose.

Japanese: 前から見て、目につくような ヘンなトコロは、なかったですな。
English: There wasn't anything that caught my interest about her when I saw her from the front.

Japanese: 被害者の岡　高夫を 見ていたワケですか？
English: So you saw the victim, then? You saw Mr. Glen Elg?

Japanese: まあ‥‥気になってはおった。 タソガレ山の全勝のゆくえは。
English: I wanted to know if Gutsen Brawn retained his championship or not.

Japanese: （関心があったのは 被害者のスポーツ新聞、か‥‥）
English: (So he was looking at the sports paper the victim was reading, huh...)

Japanese: あのレストラン‥‥
English: And at the location in question...

Japanese: テーブルのあいだに 仕切りが立っていますよね。
English: ...there are partitions between tables on the same side of the restaurant, right?

Japanese: それが、なんだ？
English: So what if there are?

Japanese: “被害者が見えた”と いうことは‥‥
English: If you say that you could see the victim...

Japanese: オジさんは、被害者の向かい側の テーブルにいたわけですか？
English: ...that means you were sitting at a table on the other side of the restaurant, correct?

Japanese: かぁッ！
English: Kah!

Japanese: ワシは、コーシーを飲むために レストランに行っておる！
English: I go to that place to drink javaccino!

Japanese: テーブルに座るためではない！ そんなの、おぼえてないワ！
English: I don't go to sit! I don't remember what table I was sitting at!

Japanese: （ウエイトレスさんを 見に行ってるくせに‥‥）
English: (You mean you go there to eye the waitress!)

Japanese: それは、重要なコトです！ まちがいありませんか？
English: Mr. Kudo. That is a very grave accusation. Are you sure about what you saw?

Japanese: この将兵、スットコチョイでも オッチョコドッコイでもないッ！
English: Victor Kudo never makes mistakes! I dot every t and cross every i!

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: I see...

Japanese: 視力だってシッカリしとる！ 両目とも“可”だ！
English: My eyesight's fine. The doctor said I only need spectacles for reading and driving.

Japanese: （‥‥いつの時代に計ったんだ？ その視力‥‥）
English: (...I bet his eyes are only really "fine" when he's scoping out a waitress.)

Japanese: 女給さんがコーシーに入れた モノも、ちゃーんと見とるワ！
English: And I saw what the serving girl put into the javaccino as well!

Japanese: （‥‥ちょっと、イヤな 予感がしないでもないな‥‥）
English: (I bet I know what's coming up and something tells me I'm not going to like it...)

Japanese: （‥‥今、ゆさぶるのは キケンだ。目をそらしておこう）
English: (It'd be dangerous to press this too much. I'd better ignore it for now.)

Japanese: それで‥‥その後、 どうなったんですか？
English: So, what happened next?

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: “コーシーに入れたモノ”とは いったい、なんだったのか‥‥
English: We need more clarification on what was put into the victim's coffee.

Japanese: 証言につけ加えてください！
English: I'd like to ask that the witness add what he knows about this to his testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: そうですね。 証人、おねがいできますかな？
English: I agree. Witness, will you enlighten us, please?

Japanese: あらよッと！
English: Sure, sure.

Japanese: ヒトクチ飲んだだけで？
English: He took just one sip?

Japanese: まったく！　もったいない コトをするワカゾウだ！
English: You young'uns! You waste everything!

Japanese: ９８０円のコーシーだぞ！
English: Those javaccinos cost 8 dollars!

Japanese: ゼンブ飲んで、コーシーの サラまで食ってから死ね！
English: In the good old days, we would have drank every last drop, eaten the cup, and then died!

Japanese: （ムチャ言ってるな‥‥）
English: (Congratulations. You have earned the title of Battiest Man To Grace A Courtroom.)

Japanese: 被害者は即死‥‥か。 青酸カリだ。‥‥ムリはねえ。
English: So it was an immediate death? Well, with potassium cyanide, I suppose that is possible.

Japanese: いきなりこう、バタッと倒れて、 動かなくなりましたぞ！
English: Oh, yes. He slumped over without so much as a twitch!

Japanese: ワシのハラの中のコーシーも 急にすっぱくなりましたワイ！
English: I felt the javaccino I'd just drank turn sour in my stomach.

Japanese: ‥‥わかるぜ。そのキモチ‥‥
English: Oh, yes. I know that feeling.

Japanese: それで、そのウエイトレスですが。 やはり‥‥？
English: And the waitress? I presume she is...?

Japanese: “よくおぼえている” ということは‥‥
English: You said, "I remember her well" in reference to the waitress...

Japanese: そのウエイトレスさん、何か トクチョウがあったんですね？
English: Did she have any particular features that you can identify her by?

Japanese: まったく、こっぱずかしい カッコウをしおって！
English: Particular features!? It's a disgrace, that's what it is!

Japanese: ‥‥はい？
English: Sorry?

Japanese: ふとももに‥‥、むむ。 その、なんだ。
English: You can see all the way up to her... her... You know!

Japanese: まるだしじゃないかッ！ どうなんだコラッ！
English: She's practically naked in that uniform!

Japanese: トクチョウ、って‥‥ 制服じゃないですか！
English: So the particular feature you recognize about the waitress is... her outfit!?

Japanese: そんなもの、ぼくにだって 着られる！
English: But anyone could wear just such an uniform! Even me!

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂くん。それはちょっと ムリがあるでしょう。
English: Mr. Wright! Please spare the court of any further mental anguish from that image.

Japanese: 落ちついて行こう、なるほどくん！
English: Don't get all excited, Nick! You've gotta keep yourself together!

Japanese: （なだめられてしまった‥‥）
English: (I-I guess I got a bit carried away...)

Japanese: かぁッ！　モチロン、他にも トクチョウはあったともさ！
English: Kah! There are other things I recognize about her, too.

Japanese: （ヨユウたっぷりだな‥‥。 どうしよう？）
English: (He seems pretty sure of himself. What should I do?)

Japanese: （‥‥もっと他に、マシな 手がかりがあるような気がする）
English: (There's got to be a better line of attack than this.)

Japanese: （それを探してみるか‥‥）
English: (That's what I should be looking for now...)

Japanese: あなたは、ウエイトレスを 見ているのかもしれません。
English: Sure, you saw a waitress take the coffee over to the victim.

Japanese: しかし！　重要なのは、それが 被告人だったかどうか、です！
English: But what matters is whether that waitress was Maggey Byrde or not.

Japanese: たしかに、そうですな。
English: Quite right.

Japanese: 証人。あなたが言う “ウエイトレスのトクチョウ”‥‥
English: Mr. Kudo. These other "features" that you recognize about the defendant...

Japanese: 証言に加えていただけますかな。
English: I would ask you to add them to your testimony.

Japanese: あらよッと！
English: Sure, sure.

Japanese: ほ、ホントにそんなものを 入れたんですか？
English: D-Did she really put that into the coffee?

Japanese: おおともサ！ 茶色の小ビンからサラサラとなァ！
English: You doubt me, boy!? She took some out of a small, brown bottle and sprinkled it in.

Japanese: き、気をきかせて、サトウを 入れたのかも‥‥
English: Couldn't she have been adding sugar?

Japanese: サトウを、あんな茶色の 小ビンに入れるかッ！
English: Sugar!? In a small, brown bottle like that!?

Japanese: しかし‥‥“あんな”小ビンとは、 どんな小ビンですかな？
English: "Like that"? Witness, could you please describe the bottle in more concrete terms?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ こんな小ビン、じゃねえかい？
English: Ha...! A bottle like this, perhaps?

Japanese: おい来た！ そんな小ビンですワ！
English: Oh, there it is! That's the one!

Japanese: これは‥‥青酸カリの 小ビン、ですか‥‥
English: That's the bottle of potassium cyanide, I presume?

Japanese: 被告人が何を入れたか‥‥？ もう、ギモンの余地はねえぜ。
English: So what did the accused put into the coffee? I think it's clear, don't you?

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Argh...

Japanese: たしかに、細かいところまで おぼえているようですが‥‥
English: You do seem to remember several details about her appearance.

Japanese: カンジンの、カオは‥‥？
English: But what about the most crucial detail of all... her face?

Japanese: かぁッ！　そんなもん、 決まっとろうが！　ワシは‥‥
English: Kah! As if I wouldn't remember that!

Japanese: この証人は、エプロンの ヒモまで見てるんだ。
English: The witness noticed the straps on the accused's apron.

Japanese: ‥‥カオだって、 まちがいねえだろうさ。
English: He's unlikely to make a mistake about her face.

Japanese: ちなみに、リボンは赤でしたな！
English: That's right! I can even tell you the color of the ribbon in her hair. It was red!

Japanese: このとおり、観察力に モンダイはねえぜ。
English: So you see, there's nothing wrong with the witness's eyesight.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: （ウエイトレスをハッキリ おぼえてるのは、まちがいない）
English: (There's no doubt he remembers the waitress pretty well.)

Japanese: （どうしよう。少し、 気になるコトがあるような‥‥）
English: (What should I do? I get the feeling there's something more to this somehow...)

Japanese: （いや‥‥。 きっと気のせいだろう）
English: (No, I must be imagining it.)

Japanese: （他の手がかりを探すんだ！）
English: (I need to find another angle with this guy.)

Japanese: 証人は‥‥ ヒモばっかり見てますね？
English: Mr. Kudo. You seem especially interested in straps. Why is that?

Japanese: な、なんだと？
English: Wh-What?

Japanese: リボンやら、エプロンのヒモやら。 ‥‥どうしてですか？
English: The ribbon in her hair, the straps on her apron. What's the fascination?

Japanese: ど、どうして、と言われても‥‥
English: F-Fascination?

Japanese: 世の中、ヘンな好みのヤツがいる。 ‥‥口出しは、ヤボだぜ。
English: People have all kinds of fetishes, Trite. We don't need to embarrass the witness.

Japanese: ヒトを“ヒモマニア”みたいに 言わないでもらいたいッ！
English: Listen, you young upstarts! I haven't got some sick strap fetish!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: この証人がヒモ好きであることに、 何か問題があるのですか？
English: Is there any relevance to the witness's unusual love of straps, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ちょっと、気になっただけです。 なぜ、そこまでヒモ好きなのか‥‥
English: I was just curious as to why he was so fixated on the waitress's straps...

Japanese: だから、ワシは ヒモ好きなんかじゃないんだッ！
English: I said I haven't got a strap fetish! How many times do I have to repeat myself!?

Japanese: まあ、いいでしょう。‥‥証人、 ヒモ以外の証言もおねがいします。
English: Very well. Continue with your testimony, Mr. Kudo. And make it strapless.

Japanese: むむむむむむ‥‥
English: *grumble grumble*

Japanese: あなたのいう“トクチョウ”‥‥ 背中から見たものばかりですね。
English: The identifying "features" you described are all things you would see from the back.

Japanese: せ、せなか‥‥？
English: So what?

Japanese: もしかして、あなたは‥‥
English: Is it possible that...

Japanese: そのウエイトレスを、 前からは見ていないのでは‥‥？
English: ...you never saw the waitress from the front at all!?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ ジイさん‥‥言われてるぜ。
English: Ha...! He's got you there, gramps!

Japanese: 人物のトクチョウを聞かれたら、 ふつうはカオのコトを言うはず。
English: People normally talk about facial features when they're asked to describe someone.

Japanese: しかし、この証人ときたら。 背中のヒモに赤いリボンです！
English: But this witness's testimony is nothing but straps and ribbons!

Japanese: ままま待てい！　ひ、ヒトを 変人みたいに言うなッ！
English: Th-This is harassment! I tell you I'm not obsessed with straps or ribbons!

Japanese: ちゃんと見ているに 決まっとろうがッ！
English: I'm just telling you what I saw!

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、証言に 加えていただきましょう。
English: Mr. Kudo, the court requests that you add details about any identifying features...

Japanese: あなたが見たウエイトレスの、 前から見たトクチョウを。
English: Features you observed from the front, that is, to your testimony.

Japanese: あらよッと！
English: Sure, sure.

Japanese: なんか、増える一方だよ。 マメのオジさんの証言‥‥。
English: This old man's testimony is getting longer and longer!

Japanese: 早くムジュンを見つけないと、 まだまだ増えるぞ、きっと。
English: And if I can't find a hole in it soon, it'll get even longer, I bet.

Japanese: それで‥‥ カオはハッキリ見たんですか？
English: You didn't find anything to be distinct, but you did clearly see the witness's face, yes?

Japanese: おおともサ！
English: No question about it!

Japanese: ここまで来て引き下がっちゃァ オトコがすたる、ってモンだ！
English: I didn't come this far to back down now! Victor Kudo never backs down!

Japanese: （そういうモンダイじゃ‥‥）
English: (That's not the answer I was looking for, but OK...)

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、イジになってるね、 マメのオジさん‥‥
English: This has turned into a matter of pride for Old Seedy now, I guess...

Japanese: ホントに、マコちゃんのカオを 見てるのかなあ。
English: I wonder if he really did see Maggey's face or not...

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥なんとかして 立証するしかない、か‥‥）
English: (Like I thought, we need some concrete proof of this.)

Japanese: （“ジイさんは、ウエイトレスを 前からキチンと見ていない”）
English: (Proof that the old guy didn't see the waitress clearly from the front.)

Japanese: 五十嵐さん。 コイツを見てください。
English: Mr. Kudo. I would like you to please take a look at this.

Japanese: かぁッ！　キサマに似合いの、 ばばっちいエプロンだな。
English: Kah! That filthy thing would suit filth like you just perfectly.

Japanese: メシを食ったあとの、 マゴのカオを思い出すワ。
English: Actually, it reminds me of what my grandson looks like, just after he's done eating.

Japanese: 見おぼえは‥‥ありますか？
English: Have you ever seen this before?

Japanese: あるワケがない！
English: Of course I haven't!

Japanese: そんなハデな前かけ、一度見たら 忘れるモンかッ！
English: Do you think I'd forget something as dirty as that!? Hmm?

Japanese: この、大たわけがッ！
English: Well? You half-witted clot!?

Japanese: な。な。なんだ！
English: Wh-What!? What is it!?

Japanese: この、“大たわけはアンタだ”と 言わんばかりの空気は‥‥
English: Ever since I said, "you half-witted clot", there's been an eerie silence in here.

Japanese: 五十嵐さん。 このエプロンは‥‥
English: Mr. Kudo. This apron...

Japanese: 事件当日、被告人がつけていた ものなんですよ。
English: ...is the apron worn by the defendant on the day of the poisoning!
Uh?

Japanese: あなたの言うとおりなんです。
English: And as you just said yourself...

Japanese: ‥‥こんなハデなエプロンを 忘れるわけがない。
English: ...you wouldn't forget something like this.

Japanese: もし、本当に それを見ていたのならば！
English: Which means, if you had really seen this apron before...
Er...

Japanese: つまり！
English: Yes, you know what I'm getting at!

Japanese: あなたは、ウエイトレスの 背中しか見ていなかったのです！
English: You couldn't have possibly seen the waitress from the front!

Japanese: こりゃマイった。
English: Oops!

Japanese: こ、コラ！　証人！
English: Witness!

Japanese: 笑ってゆるされる トシじゃありませんぞ！
English: You can't just "oops" your way out of this!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　どうやら‥‥
English: Ha...! Well, well...

Japanese: この裁判も、やっと ホンモノらしくなってきたぜ。
English: Looks like we finally have a genuine trial on our hands.

Japanese: いいか、まるほどう。 ここに１つのジジツがある。
English: Listen, Trite. Here are the facts.

Japanese: 事件当時、レストランには ウエイトレスは１人だけだった。
English: On the day of the incident, there was only one waitress in the restaurant.

Japanese: それが、被告人の須々木　マコさん ‥‥というワケですか。
English: That being the defendant, Ms. Maggey Byrde?

Japanese: そのとおりだぜ。
English: Exactly.

Japanese: その、たった１人のウエイトレスが 毒を入れた、その瞬間を‥‥
English: And when that one waitress put the poison into the coffee cup...

Japanese: ‥‥このジイさんは、 ハッキリ見ているのさ！
English: ...this old guy was watching!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 いいですかな、証人。
English: Hmm... I hope you understand the gravity of the situation, Mr. Kudo.

Japanese: 被告人のウンメイは、 あなたの記憶にかかっています。
English: The fate of the defendant may rest on what you say you remember seeing.

Japanese: ハッキリおぼえてるコトだけ、 セイカクにしゃべってくれ。
English: Just tell the court exactly what you saw, gramps.

Japanese: まかせなさい、大将！ ワシのオツムは現役ですワイ。
English: You can rely on me, Captain! My noggin's in perfect working order.

Japanese: ハンバーガー屋でも、客の注文を まちがえたコトなど、記憶がない！
English: I can't remember a single occasion when I forgot what burger a customer wanted!

Japanese: ‥‥“記憶がない”時点で、 すでにモンダイがあるような‥‥
English: He can't remember? Probably more like he messed up so many times, he's blocking it out...

Japanese: では、あなたの注意力と記憶力が セイカクかどうか、試しましょう。
English: Very well. Let's test just how good your memory and attention to detail is, Mr. Kudo.

Japanese: 今度は、あなたが見た《被害者》に ついて、詳しく聞かせてください！
English: Tell us what you remember about the victim.

Japanese: ～被害者のこと～
English: -- About the Victim --

Japanese: 気にさわるワカゾウだったですわ。 イカれたサングラスをかけて。
English: He was another of those pesky young types, wearing a broken pair of spectacles.

Japanese: ひっきりなしに、バサバサと 右の手で新聞をめくっとりました。
English: He had a newspaper in his right hand, and the noisy brat kept rustling its pages!

Japanese: それに、ラジオを聞いて おったと記憶しとります。
English: The young man was listening to the wireless. I remember that well.

Japanese: そこに、くだんの女給さんが コーシーを運んできて‥‥
English: Then the serving girl in question brought over the javaccino.

Japanese: ワカゾウは、あいてるほうの手で カップを口に運んだワケですワ。
English: The little fidget picked up the cup with his free hand and took a sip.

Japanese: この証言は、証人の記憶力を たしかめるためのものです。
English: The testimony we have just heard was to test how credible the witness's memory is.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥かなり細かいところ まで、おぼえているようですね。
English: It seems to me that he remembers the victim in a great deal of detail.

Japanese: モチロン。ワシは、あやふやな コトが大キライですからな！
English: Oh, yes! I hate those you-know-what types who are so vague about everything!

Japanese: ど、どうする？　なるほどくん！
English: How are we going to handle this, Nick?

Japanese: ぼくたちがするべきことは、 たった１つだよ。
English: We only need to do one thing.

Japanese: あのオジさんの記憶はアテに ならない。‥‥それを立証する！
English: We just need to prove that the old man's memory is shot.

Japanese: ‥‥あげ足とりみたい。 ちょっとヤだな。
English: Just trip him up, you mean? Isn't that kind of cruel?

Japanese: （ぼくは大好きだけどな‥‥）
English: (I suppose... But it's what I do best.)

Japanese: ～被害者のこと～
English: -- About the Victim --

Japanese: 気にさわるワカゾウだったですわ。 イカれたサングラスをかけて。
English: He was another of those pesky young types, wearing a broken pair of spectacles.

Japanese: ひっきりなしに、バサバサと 右の手で新聞をめくっとりました。
English: He had a newspaper in his right hand, and the noisy brat kept rustling its pages!

Japanese: それに、ラジオを聞いて おったと記憶しとります。
English: The young man was listening to the wireless. I remember that well.

Japanese: そこに、くだんの女給さんが コーシーを運んできて‥‥
English: Then the serving girl in question brought over the javaccino.

Japanese: ワカゾウは、あいてるほうの手で カップを口に運んだワケですワ。
English: The little fidget picked up the cup with his free hand and took a sip.

Japanese: （この尋問の目的は、ただ１つ）
English: (The whole point of this cross-examination is to establish just one thing...)

Japanese: （オジさんの記憶がアテにならない コトを立証する‥‥！）
English: (That this old guy's memory has more holes than a slice of Swiss.)

Japanese: そのためには‥‥やっぱり、 探すしかないよね。ムジュンを。
English: I guess we just need to find a contradiction in his testimony somewhere, huh?

Japanese: （どんなササイなことでもいい。 食いちがいを探すんだ‥‥！）
English: (Anything will do. Even the smallest detail. We just need one mistake, and he's ours!)

Japanese: サングラス‥‥？
English: Spectacles?

Japanese: ホレ。片方のレンズがコワれた、 ミドリ色のヤツ。
English: Dark glasses to you! One of the lenses was green, but the other was broken.

Japanese: アレのおかげで、イヤでも 目立ったな、あのワカゾウめ。
English: Newfangled rubbish! That's why I remember him so well.

Japanese: （‥‥たしかに、左目に何か つけているけど‥‥）
English: (He did have some kind of lens over his left eye...)

Japanese: （サングラス‥‥には 見えないな）
English: (But I wouldn't have called it a pair of glasses.)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ちょっと シャレた感じのメガネですな。
English: Hmm... He seems to have been wearing some rather modern- looking shades.

Japanese: ゴドー検事に対抗して、 私もつけてみましょうか。
English: Perhaps I should take to wearing some, and rival Mr. Godot's sharp appearance.

Japanese: お。そうなったら、 あたしたちも負けてられないね！
English: Ah! We better come up with something sharp, and quick!

Japanese: （‥‥この“メガネ”のことは、 必要になったときに聞くか‥‥）
English: (Guess I'll wait and see if I should challenge him about the "spectacles"...)

Japanese: さっき言ってた スポーツ新聞ですか。
English: The newspaper was a sports paper, was it?

Japanese: まったく！　新聞ぐらい 落ちついて読めと言いたいッ！
English: That young hooligan! I nearly asked him, "Can't you even read without fidgeting, hmm!?"

Japanese: おかげで、こっちは ちっとも集中できなかった！
English: How was I supposed to be able to read the back page with all that rustling going on!?

Japanese: タソガレ山の全勝は どうなったんだと聞きたいッ！
English: I needed to find out if Gutsen Brawn retained his championship title!

Japanese: （盗み読みしといて イバるなよ‥‥）
English: (It was his paper, not yours! If you wanted to know so bad, why didn't you buy your own!?)

Japanese: なんだコラ！　その、 ヒトをさげすんだような目はッ！
English: What are looking at me like that for, hmm!? How dare you judge me!

Japanese: いてててて！
English: Ow! Oooow!

Japanese: ちなみに、タソガレ山は、 きのうもハリ倒されてたけどね。
English: Gutsen Brawn got beaten yesterday, by the way. Anyway...

Japanese: ラジオを‥‥？
English: The wireless?

Japanese: まったく、ゼイタクな ワカゾウだッ！
English: The decadent young rascal!

Japanese: 新聞を読むかラジオを聞くか、 どっちかにしろと言いたい！
English: In my day, it was one or the other! Read the paper, or listen to the wireless!

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: (...Oh boy.)

Japanese: イヤホンなんか使いおって、 お楽しみをヒトリじめだよ。
English: And using an earpiece! It's selfish, that's what it is!

Japanese: 聞き耳を立ててみたが、 何も聞こえやせん！
English: I was straining my ears, but I couldn't catch any of it.

Japanese: （そこまでして聞きたいか？ ラジオ‥‥）
English: (Was he that desperate to listen to the radio...?)

Japanese: なんだコラ！　その、 ヒトをあわれんだような目はッ！
English: What are looking at me like that for, hmm!? How dare you feel sorry for me!

Japanese: いてててて！
English: Ow! Oooow!

Japanese: 後ろ姿しか見ていない ウエイトレス、ですね？
English: You mean the waitress, who you only saw from behind, right?

Japanese: ‥‥ちまちまと細かいオトコだな アンタは。
English: You're one of those, are you? You never let anything go, isn't that right?
...Maybe.

Japanese: 前から見ようと後ろから見ようと、 ワシの目はごまかされんワ！
English: What does it matter if I saw her from the front or from behind!? My eyes don't lie!

Japanese: いてててて！
English: Ow! Oooow!

Japanese: （決定的な証拠がないかぎり、 ケチをつけないほうがいいな）
English: (I better not push it until I've got some hard evidence.)

Japanese: “あいているほうの手”‥‥？
English: "His free hand"?

Japanese: そうだ。
English: Yes.

Japanese: ‥‥どっちの手ですか？
English: Which hand was that?

Japanese: やれやれ。ニイちゃん、 ヒトの話は聞いとくもんだ。
English: Weren't you listening before, cloth-ears!?

Japanese: さっき言ったろうが。 右手で新聞をめくってた、と！
English: I said he was rustling the newspaper with his right hand, didn't I?

Japanese: 右じゃなかったら、 どっちだコラッ！
English: If his free hand wasn't his right hand, then which hand was it, you moron!

Japanese: いてててて！
English: Ow! Oooow!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
おそらく、アンタには ３本目のウデがあるんだろうぜ‥‥
English: Perhaps the great Mr. Trite has three hands!

Japanese: （カルいキモチで聞いたのに、 カイブツあつかいされた‥‥）
English: (Yeesh! I was only asking! No need to gang up on me and treat me like a freak!)

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、須々木　マコに たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Maggey Byrde...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately...

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: ...to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!
